title: Matrix Depth-First Search
description: Given an m×n grid of 0s and 1s, count the number of distinct paths from top-left (0,0) to bottom-right (m-1,n-1). You can only move right or down through cells with value 0.
key_concept: DFS on a matrix with visited set. 4-directional or constrained traversal. Stack-based recursion, backtrack by removing from visited.
when_best: Explore all paths, find any path, connected components in grids

hints.L1: Recursive DFS: from current cell, try all valid neighbors. Mark visited to avoid cycles. Backtrack after recursion.
hints.L2: Base case: reached bottom-right → return 1. Invalid (OOB, wall, visited) → return 0. Add to visited before recursing, remove after.
hints.L3: def dfs(r,c,visit): if OOB or grid[r][c]==1 or (r,c) in visit: return 0; if r==rows-1 and c==cols-1: return 1; visit.add((r,c)); count=dfs(r+1,c)+dfs(r-1,c)+dfs(r,c+1)+dfs(r,c-1); visit.remove((r,c)); return count
